item.name: To Serve Man
entries: This newly minted book contains directions on how to prepare human flesh with a variety of gourmet treatments. This book is written in Giant and Infernal.

item.name: Book Of The World
entries: This highly oxidized tome is dated 1254 DR and provides for the legend of how metallic dragons where created by a draconic being known as "The Renegade". This book is written in Thorass, an old dialect of Common.

item.name: The Story Of Chippoccio
entries: Each handwritten page found within this wood box recounts the tale of a sentient wood golem, and it's quest to have its consciousness transferred into the body of a flesh golem. This story is written in Common.

item.name: City Of The Hammer
entries: This book is bound in stone slabs, with thin sheets of engraved mithril acting as pages, describing the location and history of the ancient dwarven city of Sarbeen. This tome is written in Dwarvish.

item.name: Underdark Edibles
entries: This book is covered in leather fashioned from the hide of a purple worm and provides a list of fully edible fungi accompanied by full color drawings. This book is written in Elvish.

item.name: Utilization Of Unicorns
entries: White leather bounds this tome, describing the various parts of a unicorn that can be used to create magic items. This tome is written in Infernal.

item.name: Castes Of Coliar
entries: The brown leather tome gilded in gold describes the populace and government systems of Coliar, the second planet from the sun in the Torilian solar system. This book is written in Gith.

item.name: Iyachtu Xvim
entries: This small, obsidian pyramid contains the microscopic writings of how to resurrect Iyachtu Xvim, the demon blooded son of Bane whom Bane consumed to bring himself back to life after the Time Of Troubles. This book is written in Celestial.

item.name: Urthag The Knife
entries: A moldy, musty tome that describes the infamous Pirate, Urthag The Knife, and his exploits of the Dragon Reach Fjord in the seventh century, DR.

item.name: Properties Of Zardazik
entries: The pages within this burned leather tome describe the properties of the rare Faerunian alloy, Zardazik. This book is written in Gnomish.

item.name: Untitled
entries: This book has a blank grey leather cover contains an Oblex spawn that attacks when the book is open.

item.name: The Galley Of The Gods
entries: This black leather book embossed with black stars gives the roster of all of the Non- Torilian Gods that crossed over to Realmspace on the Galley Of The Gods Ship. This book is written in Celestial.

item.name: The Adventures Of Zzaro
entries: This brown alcohol stained tome details the adventures of a noble of Cormyr, who believed he had super abilities when imbibing large amount of the liquor, Zzar. This book is written in Common (with a lot of spelling errors).

item.name: Shared Whispers Volume II
entries: This simple, white leather tome contains pages that have the phase "Ipsom Lorem" written in Common all throughout the book. This book has the same properties as the magic item Sending Stones as found in the Dungeon Masters Guide with the book Shared Whispers Volume I

item.name: Riddles For Dungeon Keepers
entries: {@b {@link Riddles For Dungeon Keepers|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/293836/Sublime-Riddles--Rhymes-101-Riddles-For-Your-RPG}}. {@b This book contains a number of riddles and puzzles that are meant for dungeon keepers to use when trying to stump those that would plunder said dungeon. This book is written in Common.}

item.name: Duc-Sum Li and The Lightening Dragon Dojo
entries: This is a stack of paper pamphlets that together describe how Duc-Sum Li was knighted by King Azoun V and the start of his legendary dojo near the border of Marsember.

item.name: Manipulation Of Mythals
entries: A green leather tome that describes ways to program True Mythals using modern wizardly magic. This book is written in Elvish.

item.name: Trysting Tressyms
entries: This yellow leather tome is embossed with two glass cats eyes and gives care and breeding instructions from Tressyms. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Sovereign Of Supp
entries: A book that provides rules and other details around the competitive eating even held at the Monastery Of The Yellow Rose. This book is written in common.

item.name: Rumors Across
entries: {@b {@link Rumors Across|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/233073/Gaggles-Of-Tattles-and-Scandals-1000-Rumors-For-Use-In-The-Forgotten-Realms-and-Beyond}}. {@b The Realms This humbly bound book documents various rumors and gossips found in villages thorough the Realms. This book is written in Common.}

item.name: Halruaa's Return
entries: This incomplete and battered manuscript postulates on the magic that Halruaa's wizards used to avoid the Spellplague.

item.name: Elminster's Infatuations, Volume 10
entries: This dusty tome is bound in black leather, with spider web patterns inlaid in silver. The book recounts the courtship between the famous mage and Susprina H'Unette, a prominent drow priestess in Ched Nasad, that ends in disaster. This book is written in Elvish.

item.name: Delectable Of The Dales
entries: This musty book describes the edible plants and fungi found in the Dales are of the Realms, with an emphasis on Shadowdale. This book is written in Common, but an extremely old dialect.

item.name: Noble Presentation In Waterdeep (Fall, 1379 DR)
entries: Although the patent leather cover has stood the test of time (Detect Magic will show some spell on the outer cover that prevents ageing), the inside of the pages have not fared well. What's left are artist renditions of various fashions worn by Lords and Ladies of the time. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Old Smokey
entries: This highly stained scroll of parchment provides the ingredient and directions to the famous stout of Raven's Bluff: Old Smokey. This scroll is written in Dwarvish.

item.name: Stanaz's Codex Of Exercise
entries: This 2x2 ceramic slab is a dwarf doing a number of "stoutness" exercises, including Tavern Crawls and Ale Curls. This codex is written in Dwarfish.

item.name: Consumption Of The Shadowdark
entries: This "book" actually a hand keg that contains a scroll with directions on how to brew the famous ale, Shadowdark Ale. This book is written in Common and Dwarvish.

item.name: Codex Of Fire
entries: This manual explains the proper type of wood and kindling use to start and maintain various types of fires. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Adventures of Blue Turtle and Black Turtle
entries: This is a small palm sized children's reader about the adventures of two turtles who try to climb to the top of a sand dune, teaching lessons of friendship and cooperation. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Beholding The Beholder
entries: This leather tome is embossed with a large glass eye in the middle, surrounded with 7 smaller glass eyes. It provides the lore information found in the Beholder entry of the Monster Manual (not game statistics). This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Book Of Null
entries: This book is bound in black dragon scales and gives insight into the god of dead Dragons, Null. The book goes on to explain that he is the brother of both Tiamat and Baphomet. This book is written in Draconic.

item.name: The Szarkai
entries: This is a ledger of all known Szarkai (albino drow) found throughout the Underdark, including any surface elf facades they used when spying on the surface.

item.name: My Relationship With The Goddess Shar
entries: This is a sketchpad bound in goldleaf. There is no text, with the exception of the name of the author writing his name very elegantly: Halaster Blackcloak. The remainder of the book is a collection of stick figure drawings making a variety of sad faces.

item.name: The Church Of Mystryl
entries: This perfectly square piece of electrum sheet metal has illusionary blue script that turns to its next "page" by moving one's hand over it. This book is written in Ancient Netherese.

item.name: A Halfling's Guide To Ten Towns
entries: This book is bound in well oiled fish skin and describes the best places to eat and drink at across the ten villages. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Sarkenaegal
entries: This is a black leather book with a silver skull on the cover. Almost all the pages are torn out but those that are left behind are blank, and illuminate with silver writing in perfect darkness. This was an old spell book, with the only page remaining that describes the Darkness spell. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Search For Alamion
entries: This light blue leather tome is slightly cold to the touch and acts as a diary that recounts the efforts of Nevrika Frostgate, cleric of Auril, to obtain the long lost enchanted longsword Alamion.

item.name: Dodkong
entries: This gray leather tome with a skull embossed on the cover describes the history and exploits of Dodkong, the Stonegiant Lich of the Underdark. This book written in Giant.

item.name: Mysteries Of Maegera
entries: Various pieces of orange and red leather are stitched together in a flame pattern bound this book, which describes the fire primordial that lives under the dwarven city of Gauntlgrym. This book is written in Elvish.

item.name: Water Amongst The Wastes
entries: This map is a large piece of papyrus rolled into a camel hide scroll case. It provides the location of 50 hidden Bedine water wells in the Anarouch Desert

item.name: Fare Of The Realms
entries: Red, highly worn burlap binds this book. Inside you fine recipes for various foods found around Faerun. When you touch the page, it faintly smells of the food prepared by the recipe of that page. These recipes can be found in (food of the realms link). This book is written in common.

item.name: Conquering What I Want of the World: Words For All the Brethren to Live By
entries: This small black leather tome contains the edicts of Bane as told by Fzoul Chembryl, chosen of Bane. This book is written in common.

item.name: The Beginnings Of The Hjedi
entries: The books discusses a small cabal of wizards who adopted young orphans that showed magical aptitude at a young age after the return of Halruua. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Volo's Bag Of Holding
entries: {@b {@link Volo's Bag Of Holding|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/197001/Volos-Bag-Of-Holding-1000-Trinkets-Baubles-and-Curios-Found-In-The-Forgotten-Realms-and-Beyond}}.. {@b This is a magical scroll that continues to unwind to an unnatural length to that lists all the items that Volo Geddarm has in his bag of holding. This scroll is written in Common.}

item.name: Field Study Of Saurials
entries: This book is bound in scaly brown leather and contains detailed drawing and descriptions of the various types of Saurials that occupy the region of Tarkhaldale.

item.name: The Pathway To Riches
entries: This pocket-sized book gives investing advice for merchants making a name for themselves in the Realms. It also contains a small compartment that holds 4 lock picks. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Pipes Alive
entries: This ancient tome that is clearly enchanted with preservation magic describes how to create sentient tobacco pipes. This tome is written in an ancient dialect to Dwarvish.

item.name: Stories Of The Nephalos
entries: This extremely ancient manuscript details the lives of people who are the offspring of angels and demons. This book is written half in Infernal and half in Celestial.

item.name: Demons Possessing The Dead
entries: This grey leather tome recounts circumstances where demonic entities have possessed corpses to wreak havoc in the Realms, concluding that this is the work of Dybbuks. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Roundshield's Midnight Mead
entries: This scroll is rolled up in an ornate drinking jack and provides ingredients and instructions to brew a very dark and potent dwarven mead. This scroll is written in Dwarvish.

item.name: Temples Of Erebos
entries: This red leather tome has a dragon embossed on the cover and describes the various temples of Tiamat found within the city of Erebos. This book is written in draconic.

item.name: Anatomical Studies
entries: This oversized tome details the various attempts at the creation of a species of fresh water Sahaugin conducted by the wizard Yarash of Phlan. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Ode To Cyric
entries: This black leather tome contains nothing but gibberish and nonsense written in various languages.

item.name: Whip It Good: A life In Service To Lovitar
entries: This blood red tome describes one priests life of service to the goddess Lovitar. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Untitled
entries: A small, palm sized brown leather notebook that logs a young tiefling woman's success with using herbs and poultices as contraception. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Eshowdow
entries: This book recounts the creation of the shadow giant, Eshowdow, and his attack on various tabaxi clans in Chult. This book is written in Tabaxi.

item.name: The Life of Shraevyn
entries: This very dusty and well used tome describes the life of the wizard Shraevyn, who made a variety of magic weapons for valorous adventurers during the 10 Century DR.

item.name: Nightshade Sightings
entries: This is a scroll bound with a single strap of tied leather. The scroll is a record of sightings of Nightshades, also known as Wood Woses, in the forests near Daggerdale. This book is written in Elvish.

item.name: Matrons Of Menzoberranazn
entries: This Rothe skinned book was a list of all Matrons of the eight major houses in Menzoberranzan as of 1272 DR. This book is written in Elvish. 60 Creatures to Color This light blue leather tome contains various monsters drawn using line art for children to color using chalk or water colors. The name of each creature is written in Common.

item.name: Hierarchy of The Triceratops
entries: This brown leather tome has steel triceratops on the front cover and describes the 99 layers in the membership structure of the Triceratops Society in Chult. The book is written in Common.

item.name: Ecology Of The Red Dragon
entries: A Red Leather (made from bovine skin) tome that provides the lore portion (not game statistics) of the Red Dragon Entry Of The Monster Manual. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Memories of Maerlyn
entries: This book chronicles the memories of Maerlyn Darkdance, a half elf mage who was loosing her memories during the Spellplague. This book is written in Elvish and Common and you need to speak both to get the full meaning.

item.name: Djerad Thymar
entries: This tattered brown leather tome discusses the city of Djerad Thymar in Tymanther, including a map of the city. This tome is written in Draconic.

item.name: Untitled
entries: This wax tablet can not be destroyed by magic. When any Draconic is spoken within 100 feet of this tablet, it magically scribes the translation in Common.

item.name: All About The Avowed
entries: This tan leather book describes the order of monks that maintain the great Candlekeep Library and is fully written in both Common and Infernal.

item.name: Will-O Jar Observations
entries: This 2 foot cube is a stack of wax tablets that record the observations about using jarred live Will-o-wisps to act as arcane batteries. This book is written in Common.

item.name: What We Do In The Shadows
entries: When opening this blue leather tome, one discovers that this is actually a false book that is hollowed out. The title is written in Common.

item.name: Treants Of The Forgotten Forest
entries: This series of scrolls are rolled up in order and stored in a small hollow log and provides names and personal details about the Treants that populate the Forgotten Forest. The book is written in Sylvan.

item.name: Memoirs Of The Merfolk
entries: Green leather embossed to resemble seaweed decorates this book. This documents the found of the city of Tharqualnaar near the Deep Harbor area of Waterdeep.

item.name: The Bae'qeshel Telphraezzar
entries: {@b {@link The Bae'qeshel Telphraezzar|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/179640/The-BaeQeshel-A-New-Bardic-College-For-Bards}}. {@b This book's cover consists of a series of intricate spider webs, and discusses the teachings of this ancient sect of drow bards. This book is written in Elvish.}

item.name: Herbatiousness Of The Kin
entries: This tome is stitched in petrified tobacco leaves, and has pages made of very thin paper. This book details various type of native tobacco plants found around Lurien, along with full color illustrations. This book is written in Common and Halfling.

item.name: Manaan's Journey
entries: The cover as well as the pages are made of some tanned leather skin of unknown origin, and recounts how Manaan, a servant of Asmodeus, failed to temp a mortal and "demoted" down to the form of an Imp for his failure. This book is written in Infernal.

item.name: Book Of The Crying God
entries: This stack of well stained pages bundled in blood stained cloth recounts the lore of Ilmater, the Faerunian god of suffering. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Speculations On Ibrandul
entries: This black leather tome contains theories and speculations on the return of Ibrandul, a less god of the Underdark long thought to be dead. This book is written in Undercommon.

item.name: Shared Whispers Volume I
entries: This simple, black leather tome contains pages that have the phase "Lorem Ipsom" written in Common all throughout the book. This book has the same properties as magic item Sending Stones as found in the Dungeon Masters Guide with Shared Whispers Volume II

item.name: The Diary of Jezebel Savikras
entries: This small, palm sized book recounts the pranks and practical jokes that Jezebel, Imp Familiar Extraordinaire, played on various people that lived in Balders Gate. This book is written in infernal.

item.name: Realmspace
entries: This black scroll case contains an extremely old and fragile map of the Torilian solar system. This map is written in Deep Speech.

item.name: Observations Of The Goburin
entries: This book contains the observations of a goblinoid race , Goburin, on their native prime material plane and the green moon they live on. This book is written in Ancient Ismaskari.

item.name: More Than Half Of My Love
entries: This pink colored book details the adventures dalliances of Portillo, the halfling romantic and poet. This book is written in Common.

item.name: All About Aboleth
entries: This bizarre book is bound in the skin of a shark found in the Underdark. This book contains the lore portion of the Aboleth entry of the Monster Manual (no game statistics). This book is written in Undercommon.

item.name: The Many Faces Of Manshoon
entries: This dusty ledger attempts to track how many clones of the Zhentarim Wizard Manshoon currently walk Faerun. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Studies Of The Sarrukh
entries: This book provides sketches and lore around the Sarrukh, the ancient progenitor of the Yuan-ti race and authors of the Nether Scrolls.

item.name: The Blood Of The Runt Is The Blood Of The King
entries: This book is bound in lamb skin, yet has no odor. This details the lineage of various Firbolg families found throughout the region of Hartsvale. This book is written in Giant.

item.name: The Pleasances Of Piergeiron
entries: This very frail chapbook contains a list of all the women that have shared a bed with Piergeiron the Paladinson, an Open Lord Of Waterdeep.

item.name: Automation Everywhere!
entries: A brass ringed binder with pages engraved in sheets of thin steel, providing instructions on building a variety of small mechanical animals. The chapter on building a clockwork golem is unfinished, with dried blood smears covering the last page.

item.name: Showing Dedication to Sune
entries: This fiery orange tome shows a variety of forms and poses that Priests of Sune engage in during their acts of worship. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Untitled
entries: This blue leather bound tome is a gambling ledger used to place wagers on if adventurers who enter the Blue Alley area of Waterdeep survive. This book is written in Thieves Cant.

item.name: Castle In The Clouds: A Codex
entries: This sky blue tome describes the sky castles of owned by various Cloud or Storm Giants and their most recent known whereabouts. This book is written in Giant.

item.name: Speculation On Giventhar
entries: This scroll contains the random scribbling and notes of Konavan Ebars, a noble from Amn, and his research on the location of the legendary astral blade, Giventhar.

item.name: The Scriveners Of Doom
entries: This scroll of black parchment details the caste system of The Scriveners of Doom, an order of monks dedicated to the teaching and religious practices of the god Jergal. This scroll has silver letters that will only illuminate when the scroll is opened within a mausoleum.

item.name: The Secrets Of Dumathoin
entries: This glass gem encrusted book describes various prayer and rituals related to worshipping this god. The book is written in Dwarvish.

item.name: William's Well Of Pets
entries: This simple book catalogues the number of pets that young William Offalston cared for between the ages of 5 and 10. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Ledger Of Lies
entries: This obsidian tube contains a scroll that documents the actual truth behind 100 lies told Mirt The Money Lender. The scroll is written in Common.

item.name: Harvesting The Phlogiston
entries: Purple Leather wraps this tome written in Undercommon, documenting the experiments of the Alhoon, Danviddenvap, in harvesting and using the substance as a spell component.

item.name: Bound By Our Black
entries: Blood Human skin binds this tome of religious practices by The People Of The Blackblood, Cult Of Malar composed entirely of Lycanthropes. This book is written in common.

item.name: Orcsfilth and Pixie Bane
entries: This is a small palm sized reader bound in suede contains a number of bawdy jokes and filthy stories of various length. This book is written in Common.

item.name: The Citadel Of Ice and Steel
entries: This light blue pulp-like textured pamphlet describes the capital of the plane of Elemental Air, and it's great Caliph, Husam al-Balil ben Nafhat al-Yugayyim. This book is written in Midani, Shou, and Common.

item.name: The Cup Of Nothing: A Cautionary Tale
entries: The sandy brown leather bound book describes The Cup Of Nothing: A clear skull shaped glass that magically refills itself with Moonshae Moonshine. This book is written in Common.

item.name: Maegalus
entries: This is a cobalt metal tablet contains proof of the existence of Maegalus, the child that Mystra bore out of the many unions between her and Elminster. This book illuminates purple scripting that the reader chooses and can only be read by anyone capable of casting a 9th level spell.

table.name: Books Of The Realms I

{@item A Halfling's Guide To Ten Towns|BOTR}
This book is bound in well oiled fish skin and describes the best places to eat and drink at across the ten villages. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Sarkenaegal|BOTR}

This is a black leather book with a silver skull on the cover. Almost all the pages are torn out but those that are left behind are blank, and illuminate with silver writing in perfect darkness. This was an old spell book, with the only page remaining that describes the Darkness spell. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Search For Alamion|BOTR}
This light blue leather tome is slightly cold to the touch and acts as a diary that recounts the efforts of Nevrika Frostgate, cleric of Auril, to obtain the long lost enchanted longsword Alamion.
{@item Dodkong|BOTR}
This gray leather tome with a skull embossed on the cover describes the history and exploits of Dodkong, the Stonegiant Lich of the Underdark. This book written in Giant.
{@item Mysteries Of Maegera|BOTR}

Various pieces of orange and red leather are stitched together in a flame pattern bound this book, which describes the fire primordial that lives under the dwarven city of Gauntlgrym. This book is written in Elvish.
{@item Water Amongst The Wastes|BOTR}
This map is a large piece of papyrus rolled into a camel hide scroll case. It provides the location of 50 hidden Bedine water wells in the Anarouch Desert
{@item Fare Of The Realms|BOTR}

Red, highly worn burlap binds this book. Inside you fine recipes for various foods found around Faerun. When you touch the page, it faintly smells of the food prepared by the recipe of that page. These recipes can be found in (food of the realms link). This book is written in common.
{@item Conquering What I Want of the World: Words For All the Brethren to Live By|BOTR}
This small black leather tome contains the edicts of Bane as told by Fzoul Chembryl, chosen of Bane. This book is written in common.
{@item The Beginnings Of The Hjedi|BOTR}
The books discusses a small cabal of wizards who adopted young orphans that showed magical aptitude at a young age after the return of Halruua. This book is written in Common.
{@item Volo's Bag Of Holding|BOTR}

{@b {@link Volo's Bag Of Holding|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/197001/Volos-Bag-Of-Holding-1000-Trinkets-Baubles-and-Curios-Found-In-The-Forgotten-Realms-and-Beyond}}. {@b This is a magical scroll that continues to unwind to an unnatural length to that lists all the items that Volo Geddarm has in his bag of holding. This scroll is written in Common.}
{@item Field Study Of Saurials|BOTR}
This book is bound in scaly brown leather and contains detailed drawing and descriptions of the various types of Saurials that occupy the region of Tarkhaldale.
{@item The Pathway To Riches|BOTR}
This pocket-sized book gives investing advice for merchants making a name for themselves in the Realms. It also contains a small compartment that holds 4 lock picks. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Pipes Alive|BOTR}
This ancient tome that is clearly enchanted with preservation magic describes how to create sentient tobacco pipes. This tome is written in an ancient dialect to Dwarvish.
{@item Stories Of The Nephalos|BOTR}
This extremely ancient manuscript details the lives of people who are the offspring of angels and demons. This book is written half in Infernal and half in Celestial.
{@item Demons Possessing The Dead|BOTR}
This grey leather tome recounts circumstances where demonic entities have possessed corpses to wreak havoc in the Realms, concluding that this is the work of Dybbuks. This book is written in Common.
{@item Roundshield's Midnight Mead|BOTR}
This scroll is rolled up in an ornate drinking jack and provides ingredients and instructions to brew a very dark and potent dwarven mead. This scroll is written in Dwarvish.
{@item Temples Of Erebos|BOTR}
This red leather tome has a dragon embossed on the cover and describes the various temples of Tiamat found within the city of Erebos. This book is written in draconic.
{@item Anatomical Studies|BOTR}
This oversized tome details the various attempts at the creation of a species of fresh water Sahaugin conducted by the wizard Yarash of Phlan. This book is written in Common.
{@item Ode To Cyric|BOTR}
This black leather tome contains nothing but gibberish and nonsense written in various languages.
{@item Whip It Good: A life In Service To Lovitar|BOTR}
This blood red tome describes one priests life of service to the goddess Lovitar. This book is written in Common.
{@item Untitled|BOTR}
A small, palm sized brown leather notebook that logs a young tiefling woman's success with using herbs and poultices as contraception. This book is written in Common.
{@item Eshowdow|BOTR}
This book recounts the creation of the shadow giant, Eshowdow, and his attack on various tabaxi clans in Chult. This book is written in Tabaxi.
{@item The Life of Shraevyn|BOTR}
This very dusty and well used tome describes the life of the wizard Shraevyn, who made a variety of magic weapons for valorous adventurers during the 10 Century DR.
{@item Nightshade Sightings|BOTR}

This is a scroll bound with a single strap of tied leather. The scroll is a record of sightings of Nightshades, also known as Wood Woses, in the forests near Daggerdale. This book is written in Elvish.
{@item Matrons Of Menzoberranazn|BOTR}

This Rothe skinned book was a list of all Matrons of the eight major houses in Menzoberranzan as of 1272 DR. This book is written in Elvish. 60 Creatures to Color This light blue leather tome contains various monsters drawn using line art for children to color using chalk or water colors. The name of each creature is written in Common.
{@item Hierarchy of The Triceratops|BOTR}
This brown leather tome has steel triceratops on the front cover and describes the 99 layers in the membership structure of the Triceratops Society in Chult. The book is written in Common.
{@item Ecology Of The Red Dragon|BOTR}
A Red Leather (made from bovine skin) tome that provides the lore portion (not game statistics) of the Red Dragon Entry Of The Monster Manual. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Memories of Maerlyn|BOTR}

This book chronicles the memories of Maerlyn Darkdance, a half elf mage who was loosing her memories during the Spellplague. This book is written in Elvish and Common and you need to speak both to get the full meaning.
{@item Djerad Thymar|BOTR}
This tattered brown leather tome discusses the city of Djerad Thymar in Tymanther, including a map of the city. This tome is written in Draconic.
{@item Untitled|BOTR}
This wax tablet can not be destroyed by magic. When any Draconic is spoken within 100 feet of this tablet, it magically scribes the translation in Common.
{@item All About The Avowed|BOTR}
This tan leather book describes the order of monks that maintain the great Candlekeep Library and is fully written in both Common and Infernal.
{@item Will-O Jar Observations|BOTR}
This 2 foot cube is a stack of wax tablets that record the observations about using jarred live Will-o-wisps to act as arcane batteries. This book is written in Common.
{@item What We Do In The Shadows|BOTR}
When opening this blue leather tome, one discovers that this is actually a false book that is hollowed out. The title is written in Common.
{@item Treants Of The Forgotten Forest|BOTR}

This series of scrolls are rolled up in order and stored in a small hollow log and provides names and personal details about the Treants that populate the Forgotten Forest. The book is written in Sylvan.
{@item Memoirs Of The Merfolk|BOTR}
Green leather embossed to resemble seaweed decorates this book. This documents the found of the city of Tharqualnaar near the Deep Harbor area of Waterdeep.
{@item The Bae'qeshel Telphraezzar|BOTR}

{@b {@link The Bae'qeshel Telphraezzar|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/179640/The-BaeQeshel-A-New-Bardic-College-For-Bards}}. {@b This book's cover consists of a series of intricate spider webs, and discusses the teachings of this ancient sect of drow bards. This book is written in Elvish.}
{@item Herbatiousness Of The Kin|BOTR}

This tome is stitched in petrified tobacco leaves, and has pages made of very thin paper. This book details various type of native tobacco plants found around Lurien, along with full color illustrations. This book is written in Common and Halfling.
{@item Manaan's Journey|BOTR}

The cover as well as the pages are made of some tanned leather skin of unknown origin, and recounts how Manaan, a servant of Asmodeus, failed to temp a mortal and "demoted" down to the form of an Imp for his failure. This book is written in Infernal.
{@item Book Of The Crying God|BOTR}
This stack of well stained pages bundled in blood stained cloth recounts the lore of Ilmater, the Faerunian god of suffering. This book is written in Common.
{@item Speculations On Ibrandul|BOTR}
This black leather tome contains theories and speculations on the return of Ibrandul, a less god of the Underdark long thought to be dead. This book is written in Undercommon.
{@item Shared Whispers Volume I|BOTR}

This simple, black leather tome contains pages that have the phase "Lorem Ipsom" written in Common all throughout the book. This book has the same properties as magic item Sending Stones as found in the Dungeon Masters Guide with Shared Whispers Volume II
{@item The Diary of Jezebel Savikras|BOTR}
This small, palm sized book recounts the pranks and practical jokes that Jezebel, Imp Familiar Extraordinaire, played on various people that lived in Balders Gate. This book is written in infernal.
{@item Realmspace|BOTR}
This black scroll case contains an extremely old and fragile map of the Torilian solar system. This map is written in Deep Speech.
{@item Observations Of The Goburin|BOTR}
This book contains the observations of a goblinoid race , Goburin, on their native prime material plane and the green moon they live on. This book is written in Ancient Ismaskari.
{@item More Than Half Of My Love|BOTR}
This pink colored book details the adventures dalliances of Portillo, the halfling romantic and poet. This book is written in Common.
{@item All About Aboleth|BOTR}

This bizarre book is bound in the skin of a shark found in the Underdark. This book contains the lore portion of the Aboleth entry of the Monster Manual (no game statistics). This book is written in Undercommon.
{@item The Many Faces Of Manshoon|BOTR}
This dusty ledger attempts to track how many clones of the Zhentarim Wizard Manshoon currently walk Faerun. This book is written in Common.
{@item Studies Of The Sarrukh|BOTR}
This book provides sketches and lore around the Sarrukh, the ancient progenitor of the Yuan-ti race and authors of the Nether Scrolls.
{@item The Blood Of The Runt Is The Blood Of The King|BOTR}
This book is bound in lamb skin, yet has no odor. This details the lineage of various Firbolg families found throughout the region of Hartsvale. This book is written in Giant.
{@item The Pleasances Of Piergeiron|BOTR}
This very frail chapbook contains a list of all the women that have shared a bed with Piergeiron the Paladinson, an Open Lord Of Waterdeep.
{@item Automation Everywhere!|BOTR}

A brass ringed binder with pages engraved in sheets of thin steel, providing instructions on building a variety of small mechanical animals. The chapter on building a clockwork golem is unfinished, with dried blood smears covering the last page.
{@item Showing Dedication to Sune|BOTR}
This fiery orange tome shows a variety of forms and poses that Priests of Sune engage in during their acts of worship. This book is written in Common.
{@item Untitled|BOTR}
This blue leather bound tome is a gambling ledger used to place wagers on if adventurers who enter the Blue Alley area of Waterdeep survive. This book is written in Thieves Cant.
{@item Castle In The Clouds: A Codex|BOTR}
This sky blue tome describes the sky castles of owned by various Cloud or Storm Giants and their most recent known whereabouts. This book is written in Giant.
{@item Speculation On Giventhar|BOTR}
This scroll contains the random scribbling and notes of Konavan Ebars, a noble from Amn, and his research on the location of the legendary astral blade, Giventhar.
{@item The Scriveners Of Doom|BOTR}

This scroll of black parchment details the caste system of The Scriveners of Doom, an order of monks dedicated to the teaching and religious practices of the god Jergal. This scroll has silver letters that will only illuminate when the scroll is opened within a mausoleum.
{@item The Secrets Of Dumathoin|BOTR}
This glass gem encrusted book describes various prayer and rituals related to worshipping this god. The book is written in Dwarvish.
{@item William's Well Of Pets|BOTR}
This simple book catalogues the number of pets that young William Offalston cared for between the ages of 5 and 10. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Ledger Of Lies|BOTR}
This obsidian tube contains a scroll that documents the actual truth behind 100 lies told Mirt The Money Lender. The scroll is written in Common.
{@item Harvesting The Phlogiston|BOTR}
Purple Leather wraps this tome written in Undercommon, documenting the experiments of the Alhoon, Danviddenvap, in harvesting and using the substance as a spell component.
{@item Bound By Our Black|BOTR}
Blood Human skin binds this tome of religious practices by The People Of The Blackblood, Cult Of Malar composed entirely of Lycanthropes. This book is written in common.
{@item Orcsfilth and Pixie Bane|BOTR}
This is a small palm sized reader bound in suede contains a number of bawdy jokes and filthy stories of various length. This book is written in Common.
{@item The Citadel Of Ice and Steel|BOTR}

This light blue pulp-like textured pamphlet describes the capital of the plane of Elemental Air, and it's great Caliph, Husam al-Balil ben Nafhat al-Yugayyim. This book is written in Midani, Shou, and Common.
{@item The Cup Of Nothing: A Cautionary Tale|BOTR}
The sandy brown leather bound book describes The Cup Of Nothing: A clear skull shaped glass that magically refills itself with Moonshae Moonshine. This book is written in Common.
{@item Maegalus|BOTR}

This is a cobalt metal tablet contains proof of the existence of Maegalus, the child that Mystra bore out of the many unions between her and Elminster. This book illuminates purple scripting that the reader chooses and can only be read by anyone capable of casting a 9th level spell.
